project.name: DORA Settlement-Layer Attestation Gate
project.description:
Settlement-rail enforcement component for DORA-compliant payment infrastructure. Sits at the central-bank settlement layer (RIX-INST, TIPS, FedNow or equivalent) and performs deterministic cryptographic signature verification at settlement time. Companion artefact to the hsm reference (https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19930311) and the gatekeeper reference (https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19930396).
Scope: For each settlement request identified as a regulated payment (Swish utbetalning in the Swedish reference deployment, identified via PmtTpInf/LclInstrm or via the structural derivation OrgId(debtor) + PrvtId(creditor)), railgate orchestrates:
(1) retrieval of the SHA-512 digest, RSA signature, and certificate serial number from the payment-network operator (Getswish AB in the Swedish deployment) — the digest, not the payload, satisfies GDPR Art 5(1)(c) data minimisation;
(2) cryptographic verification at the supervisor's gatekeeper instance via POST /api/v1/verify, returning a binary {signature_valid, compliant} decision derived from the stored certificate and audit-log entry;
(3) default-deny enforcement: settlements without a valid cryptographic chain to a compliant gatekeeper audit entry are blocked at RIX-INST level, returning a structured error code to the originating bank.
Data minimisation: railgate never sees, transports, or stores transaction payload content (amounts, sender/receiver detail, business message). It handles only cryptographic artefacts (digest, signature, cert serial) and a binary verification outcome. This satisfies GDPR Art 5(1)(c) and the proportionality requirement implicit in DORA Art 32 supervisory data processing.
Architectural role: The triple hsm + gatekeeper + railgate operationalises the quadruple-triangulation model described in the companion academic article. Each component answers one specific question:
hsm — is the key HSM-bound and on-device generated? gatekeeper — is the certificate-issuance compliant with structural-independence requirements, and does the signature verify against the stored public key? railgate — is settlement permitted, given the gatekeeper response and the absence of any circumvention path at the settlement rail?
Legal basis (Union law): DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — Articles 6.4, 6.6, 6.10, 9, 28.4(e), 28(7), 30, 32 (oversight forum), 33(3) (assessment criteria), 35–42 (supervisory powers). eIDAS Regulation (EU) 910/2014 — Articles 24–32 (qualified electronic signatures and signature creation devices). PSD2 Directive (EU) 2015/2366 — Articles 35–36 (non-discriminatory access to payment systems). GDPR Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — Article 5(1)(c) (data minimisation).
Legal basis (Swedish national law): Lag (1988:1385) om Sveriges riksbank — Riksbankens uppdrag avseende betalningssystem (RIX-INST). Lag (2010:751) om betaltjänster — 8 kap. tillsyn av betaltjänstleverantörer. Förordning (2009:93) med instruktion för Finansinspektionen.
License: MIT.
